Hematopoiesis
The formation and differentiation of blood cellss

Stem Cells and Hematopoiesis
Irving Weissmann
Weissman (2000)

Note: this is in humans
Sites of stem cell activity

Hematopoietic stem cells (HSC)
-scarce, 1 in 50,000
-can transplant these and repopulate a 			destroyed immune system
-are the basis for some immune therapy

Dividing HSC have four developmental fates:
Self renewal
Differentiation
Emigration
Programmed cell death

Leukemias
Anemia
Immune deficiency disorders
Some solid-tumor cancers
HSC transplantation is often used to treat:

Hematopoietic  Stem cell lineage ala Irv
LT=Long term   ST=short term       MPP=multi potent progenitor
CMP-Common myeloid progenitor
CLP-Common lymphoid progenitor

Blood cell lineages-Cells of the Immune system
Note:
This is not a complete list of the cells you need to know
Note:
leukocytes are white blood cells

Hematopoiesis
-in humans the location changes through development
-these cells are relatively scarce
-can be used to repopulate a bone marrow depleted system
-can repopulate with genetically modified cells
-can be isolated with panning
-efficient production uses negative and positive selection
-HSC often isolated from hip in adults

“However, we currently have an insufficient understanding of the developmental events that lead to organogenesis from ICM cells to program the production of tissue-or organ-specific stem cells.”
Weissman

Distinguishing Features of Progenitor/Precursor Cells and Stem Cells. A stem cell is an unspecialized cell that is capable of replicating or self renewing itself and developing into specialized cells of a variety of cell types. The product of a stem cell undergoing division is at least one additional stem cell that has the same capabilities of the originating cell. Shown here is an example of a hematopoietic stem cell producing a second generation stem cell and a neuron. A progenitor cell (also known as a precursor cell) is unspecialized or has partial characteristics of a specialized cell that is capable of undergoing cell division and yielding two specialized cells. Shown here is an example of a myeloid progenitor/precursor undergoing cell division to yield two specialized cells (a neutrophil and a red blood cell).

Hematopoietic  Stem cell lineage ala Irv
LT=Long term           ST=short term
MPP=multi potent progenitor
Blood cell lineages-Cells of the Immune system
